name: Fangar Bay and Lighthouse Trail
name_original: Parque Natural del Delta de l'Ebre – vuelta por el Faro del Fangar
description: Explore the unique "desert of the Delta del Ebro" on this 19.6-mile mountain bike route to the iconic Fangar Lighthouse.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around L'Ampolla offer a diverse range of terrain, from the flat, aquatic landscapes of the Ebro Delta to more challenging coastal and inland routes. The region is characterized by extensive rice fields, lagoons, and riparian forests within the UNESCO Biosphere Reserve of the Ebro Delta. Along the coast, trails feature cliffs, coves, and beaches, providing varied scenery. Further inland, more elevated terrain offers challenging rides with significant climbs. Best mountain bike trails around L'Ampolla The most popular mountain bike trail is Fangar Bay and Lighthouse Trail, a 19.6 miles (31.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour 16 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views across Fangar Bay towards its iconic lighthouse. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Flamingos in the Ebro Delta – Ebro Delta loop from L'Ampolla - Perelló - Deltebre, an easy 18.6 miles (29.9 km) path. This route winds through the flat, biodiverse landscapes of the Ebro Delta, known for its birdlife. Local mountain bikers also love the Garratxal Lighthouse – Ebro Delta Viewpoint loop from L'Ampolla - Perelló - Deltebre, a 38.1 miles (61.4 km) trail leading through coastal and delta scenery, often completed in about 3 hours 48 minutes. Mountain biking around L'Ampolla is defined by the flat, wetland terrain of the Ebro Delta, rugged coastal paths, and accessible mountainous backdrops. The network offers options for easy rides through nature reserves as well as more challenging routes with elevation gains. The routes in L'Ampolla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore L'Ampolla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around L'Ampolla?
A: There are nearly 40 mountain bike trails around L'Ampolla, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include routes through the flat landscapes of the Ebro Delta, scenic coastal paths, and more challenging inland trails.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in L'Ampolla?
A: Yes, L'Ampolla offers 9 easy mountain bike trails, primarily found within the Ebro Delta Natural Park. These routes are generally flat and well-signposted, perfect for those new to mountain biking or looking for a relaxed ride. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14890069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Flamingos in the Ebro Delta – Ebro Delta loop from L'Ampolla&lt;/a&gt;, which winds through biodiverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in L'Ampolla?
A: The terrain around L'Ampolla is highly varied. You'll find extensive flat routes through the rice fields, lagoons, and riparian forests of the Ebro Delta. Along the coast, trails feature stunning cliffs, coves, and beaches. Further inland, there are more elevated and challenging routes with significant climbs, offering a different kind of adventure.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails offer views of the Ebro Delta's unique aquatic habitats, including lagoons like the Bassa de les Olles, known for birdwatching. Along the coast, you can discover stunning coves such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/566139" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala de Santes Creus&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4567455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cala Maria&lt;/a&gt;, as well as the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/802480" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fangar Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also lead past historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4567270" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fortifications of Punta de l'Àliga&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, L'Ampolla has 4 difficult mountain bike trails for experienced riders seeking a challenge. These routes often venture into the more elevated inland terrain, offering significant climbs and descents. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23755627" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Perelló Windmill loop from L'Ampolla&lt;/a&gt;, which features over 800 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that are suitable for families?
A: Absolutely. The flat, well-signposted routes within the Ebro Delta Natural Park are ideal for families. These easy trails allow for a relaxed pace, perfect for enjoying the scenery and wildlife together. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14890069" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Flamingos in the Ebro Delta loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great family-friendly option.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in L'Ampolla?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally considered the best for mountain biking in L'Ampolla. The weather is milder, making for more comfortable rides. During these seasons, the Ebro Delta is particularly vibrant with birdlife, and the coastal paths offer pleasant temperatures.

Q: Can I rent a mountain bike in L'Ampolla or the Ebro Delta area?
A: Yes, bike rental services are available in the Ebro Delta, including in nearby towns like Sant Jaume d'Enveja. This makes it convenient for visitors to explore the region's diverse trails without bringing their own equipment.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in L'Ampolla?
A: The mountain bike trails in L'Ampolla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 20 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse scenery, from the unique Ebro Delta landscapes and abundant birdlife to the stunning coastal views and challenging inland climbs. The variety of routes catering to all skill levels is also a frequently highlighted positive aspect.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that are circular (loop trails) in L'Ampolla?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around L'Ampolla are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950567957" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fangar Bay and Lighthouse Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38154621" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Garratxal Lighthouse – Ebro Delta Viewpoint loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offer varied scenery and return you to your starting point.

Q: Is there public transport available to access mountain bike trails in L'Ampolla?
A: L'Ampolla is accessible by public transport, particularly by train, which can be useful for reaching the town. However, accessing specific trailheads within the Ebro Delta or more remote coastal/inland areas might require further planning or local transport options, as direct public transport to all starting points may be limited.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for mountain biking in the Ebro Delta Natural Park?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for mountain biking on designated trails within the Ebro Delta Natural Park. However, it's always advisable to stay on marked paths to protect the sensitive ecosystem and respect local regulations, especially in protected areas known for bird nesting or other wildlife.
tips: Very long and quiet beach located to the south of the Fangar peninsula, characteristic for having some of the best preserved fine sand dunes in the Delta. In addition, the beach offers an excellent perspective of the coastline.

Luminous tower that indicates to ships the proximity of Punta del Fangar, an extensive coastal peninsula that includes sandbanks and dunes. The lighthouse is visible from much of the Ebro Delta and is one of the architectural and landscape symbols of Terres de l'Ebre.
Viewpoint with views of the shallow lagoons used in the water decontamination process in a natural and passive way thanks to plants, microorganisms and sediments.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the mountain bike route to Faro del Fangar?
faqs.answer: The route is predominantly flat, traversing dirt paths, secondary roads, and dedicated bike lanes. A significant portion leads along the beach. Be aware that the final 4 km stretch directly to the Fangar Lighthouse is often soft sand, which can be challenging and may require walking your bike. This section can also be affected by tides.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The estimated riding time for this 31.6 km route is around 1 hour and 16 minutes. However, this is a riding-only estimate. Due to the challenging sandy sections near the Fangar Lighthouse and opportunities for birdwatching or enjoying the scenery, it's advisable to allow more time, potentially 2-3 hours, especially if you plan to stop frequently.

faqs.question: Is the Parque Natural del Delta de l'Ebre – vuelta por el Faro del Fangar route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While Komoot categorizes this mountain bike route as moderate, it has minimal elevation gain and is generally considered suitable for all fitness levels. However, some cycling experience is beneficial, particularly for navigating the soft sandy sections leading to the Fangar Lighthouse, which can increase the technical difficulty and physical effort required.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The most iconic feature is the Fangar Lighthouse, with its distinctive white and red stripes. Other highlights include the Playa de la Marquesa and the Viewpoint of Illa de Mar Wetland, which is excellent for birdwatching. The route also offers expansive views of the Mediterranean Sea and the unique 'desert-like' environment of Punta del Fangar.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Ebro Delta experiences mild winters and hot summers. Spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) are generally the best times to visit for mountain biking, offering pleasant temperatures and ideal conditions for birdwatching. Summers can be very hot, making the ride more challenging.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: As this route is within the Parque Natural del Delta de l'Ebre, dogs are generally permitted but must be kept on a leash to protect the local wildlife and natural environment. Always ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or have to pay an entrance fee for the Natural Park?
faqs.answer: There is no entrance fee to access the Parque Natural del Delta de l'Ebre or to cycle this specific route. However, it is a protected natural area, so visitors are expected to respect all regulations regarding wildlife, waste, and designated paths.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas are not detailed, many visitors start near the Playa de la Marquesa or nearby towns like Deltebre, where you can find public parking. It's advisable to check local parking options in advance, especially during peak season.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for sandy sections, it's recommended to bring plenty of water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), basic bike repair tools (patch kit, pump), and good tires. Binoculars are also a great idea for birdwatching at the lagoons and viewpoints.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with sections of other official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Ecomuseu - Badia del Fangar, Filtre d'Illa de Mar, Catalunya E30, and Camí del Mas Vinyes trails during your ride.

faqs.question: Is there an insider tip for tackling the sandy section to the lighthouse?
faqs.answer: The 4 km sandy stretch to the Fangar Lighthouse is the most challenging part. An insider tip is to check tide times before you go; riding closer to low tide might offer firmer sand. Alternatively, be prepared to walk your bike for this section, and consider it part of the adventure through the Delta's unique 'desert' landscape.

waypoints.name: Viewpoint of Illa de Mar Wetland
